text: Traditional periodization cannot reliably produce multiple performance peaks within a single competitive season.
Long-duration mixed training programs that develop many abilities simultaneously produce insufficient training stimuli for highly qualified athletes to continue progressing.
Combining non-compatible workloads within the same training phase induces conflicting training responses that negatively interact and limit adaptation.

Block periodization uses specialized mesocycle-blocks with highly concentrated workloads focused on a minimal number of motor and technical abilities, rather than developing many abilities simultaneously.
Effective block periodization depends on rational sequencing of specialized mesocycle-blocks that exploits and superimposes residual training effects from preceding blocks.
Different types of mesocycle-blocks may be suited to different biological adaptation mechanisms, including homeostatic regulation and the general adaptation syndrome.
